Fury (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $85,817,906Details
International Box Office $124,403,102
Worldwide Box Office $210,221,008
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $18,131,988
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $17,752,124
Total $35,884,112
Further financial details...

Synopsis

April, 1945. As the Allies make their final push in the European Theatre, a battle-hardened army sergeant named Wardaddy commands a Sherman tank and her five-man crew on a deadly mission behind enemy lines. Outnumbered and outgunned, and with a rookie soldier thrust into their platoon, Wardaddy and his men face overwhelming odds in their heroic attempts to strike at the heart of Nazi Germany.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$23,702,421 (27.6% of total gross)
Legs:3.62 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:40.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$80,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.6 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,173 opening theaters/3,313 max. theaters, 6.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $125,602,281

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 17th, 2014 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: October 23rd, 2014 (Wide) (New Zealand)
October 23rd, 2014 (Wide) (Australia)
November 14th, 2014 (Wide) (Taiwan)
November 20th, 2014 (Wide) (South Korea)
January 1st, 2015 (Wide) (Mexico)
Video Release: January 27th, 2015 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for strong sequences of war violence, some grisly images, and language throughout.
(Rating bulletin 2337, 8/27/2014)
Running Time: 134 minutes
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Social setting: Behind Enemy Lines, World War II
Subgenre: War Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Historical Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: QED International, Le Grisbi, Crave Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

April, 1945. As the Allies make their final push in the European Theatre, a battle-hardened army sergeant named Wardaddy commands a Sherman tank and her five-man crew on a deadly mission behind enemy lines. Outnumbered and outgunned, and with a rookie soldier thrust into their platoon, Wardaddy and his men face overwhelming odds in their heroic attempts to strike at the heart of Nazi Germany.
